content: You need a screenshot utility. A super FREE one is at https://www.reasyze.com/, called sniptool. A fancier one I like, that is also free, but requires a modest learning curve, is at https://portableapps.com/apps/graphics_pictures/fscportable
creator: davidsk

content:
You can't move that line. The headers pane is of fixed size. However, there's a scroll bar at the right so you can view all the headers. You might also be able to scroll with the mouse wheel.

I wonder if you really want to display all headers? Most of them are not useful in everyday life. Unless you have a good reason, you might be more comfortable with the "Normal Headers" setting. This will display only the most useful header, plus they will all fit inside the Headers pane without needing to scroll. See menu item <View | Headers>.
creator: rednoise

content: I'm guessing you accidentally marked them as Read. You can toggle one or more selected messages between Read and Unread status with the "M" key. There are also some other keystrokes that can mark or unmark them - see menu item <Messages | Mark>.
creator: rednoise

content:
Thunderbird should be using the authentication method oauth, not password of any sort for gmail.

This process has some requirements that those that change default security settings tend to crash into. See https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/automatic-conversion-google-mail-accounts-oauth20
creator: MattAuSupport

content:
Per the instruction in link: https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/automatic-conversion-google-mail-accounts-oauth20

  • Ensure that your Thunderbird Gmail account is set to OAuth2 – YES, for both incoming and outgoing servers.
  • Cookies and JavaScript: Both are enabled.
  • Check if the proper authentication tokens have been saved: None are saved.
  • imap://imap.gmail.com (imap://imap.gmail.com): Not saved
  • smtp://smtp.gmail.com (smtp://smtp.gmail.com): Not Saved

it still fails with an error message: "Unable to login at the server. Probably wrong configuration, username or password."

content:
If installed from thunderbird.net, the profile will be a tc:\users\<yourid>\appdata\roaming\thunderbird BUT, if you installed from Microsoft, the profile is at C:\Users\<Yourid>\AppData\Local\Packages\MozillaThunderbird.MZLA

There is scant documentation on the Microsoft version.Going forward, I suggest always installing from thunderbird.net
creator: davidsk

content:
I have filled in an un-correct password a number of times due to incorrect language setting of my keyboard.

When did you type the incorrect password? When Thunderbird prompted you for the password, or when you set the password on the email provider's server?

Then the popup window for this disappeared

What pop-up exactly?

I cannot find a place to enter password.

Try to delete the password Thunderbird may have remembered for the troubled account. Then Thunderbird will prompt you for the password when it's needed. At the top right of the Thunderbird window, click the menu button ≡ > Settings > Privacy & Security > Passwords > Saved Passwords
creator: christ1

content:
I believe I have some insight on this issue. I have been working for weeks to setup an account for a defunct comcast.net email address that was taken over by Yahoo. I found the following in another report:

"Try to delete the password Thunderbird may have remembered for the troubled account. Then Thunderbird will prompt you for the password when it's needed. At the top right of the Thunderbird window, click the menu button ≡ > Settings > Privacy & Security > Passwords > Saved Passwords"

When I followed the instructions the "Saved Passwords" popup showed the provider account, the email name, and a date, which I surmise is the creation date. Could it be that Thunderbird stores the passwords by provider? That might explain why I get "Authentication error" when I try to save an account such as "lastnamefirstinitial@comcast.net" as a yahoo account; it's using the yahoo.com password which is not correct for the comcast.net account on Yahoo. If so, I think Thunderbird needs to store passwords according to the email address instead of the provider account. I hope that helps.
creator: norisezp

content: POP3 permette di scaricare localmente solo la posta che in Gmail corrisponde Posta in arrivo. Se i messaggi arrivano in un'altra posizione o se vengono letti da un'altra postazione (dal browser, dallo smartphone, da un'app) non vengono più scaricati. Potresti rientrare in uno di questi casi?
